questionText: Decide which claim about this line graph of stickers earned on a treasure hunt is true. The graph shows Monday 8, Tuesday 13, Wednesday 10, Thursday 16, and Friday 14 stickers. One note says, "The graph ends 6 stickers higher than it starts, so the total of all the increases is 6."
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can interpret data and identify trends in a line graph
issues: [difficulty_mismatch, attribution_framing, distractor_quality] | The item is labeled hard, but the task is only a simple claim check with straightforward reading of two increases (5 and 6), so the cognitive demand is below hard. | The stem uses attribution framing ('One note says...'), which should be flagged per the review rules. | One distractor is weak/implausible: 'adding 13 to 16 and subtracting 10 and 14' is not a realistic grade-4 misconception and feels arbitrary rather than based on student thinking. | Fix: Relabel the item as medium, or increase complexity to make it truly hard by requiring multi-step reasoning in a richer context or comparing multiple claims/errors. | Fix: Rewrite the stem without attribution framing, e.g. ask directly whether the statement about total increases is true and why. | Fix: Replace the arbitrary distractor with a realistic misconception, such as confusing net change (6) with total increase (11) or counting only one increase.
